steps.prompt: Which word is the main verb?

hints.promissum: 'Promissum' is a noun/participle meaning 'the promise' — the direct object
hints.ego: 'Ego' is a personal pronoun meaning 'I' — the subject
hints.Patris: 'Patris' is a noun in the genitive meaning 'of the Father'

steps.reveal: mitto = I send / I am sending (1st sing. pres.)

hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'mittam' — 'mitto' is indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'mittam' — in 3rd conjugation, future and present subjunctive look similar, but 'mitto' is present indicative

hints.misceo, miscere, miscui, mixtum: These are the parts of 'misceo' (to mix)
hints.moneo, monere, monui, monitum: These are the parts of 'moneo' (to warn)
hints.moveo, movere, movi, motum: These are the parts of 'moveo' (to move)

steps.reveal: promissum = the promise (acc., direct object of 'mitto')

hints.nominative: 'Promissum' looks the same in nominative neuter, but here it is the object of 'mitto'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'promissi' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'promissi' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'promissi' ends in -i

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'patris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'patris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -us — 'patris' ends in -is

steps.reveal: in vos = upon you (acc., 'in' + acc. = motion toward)

hints.nominative: Nominative 'vos' looks the same, but 'in' + acc. = upon/toward
hints.ablative: Ablative of 'vos' is 'vobis' — 'vos' after 'in' (motion) is accusative
hints.dative: Dative of 'vos' is 'vobis' — 'vos' is accusative after 'in'

steps.prompt: What type of genitive is 'Patris' in 'promissum Patris'?
steps.target: subjective genitive (the Father made the promise)
choices: subjective genitive (the Father made the promise)
steps.reveal: promissum Patris = the promise [made by] the Father

hints.objective genitive: Objective genitive would mean the promise is about the Father — here the Father is the one promising
hints.partitive genitive: Partitive genitive means 'part of' — 'Patris' identifies the promiser
hints.genitive of description: Genitive of description qualifies a characteristic — 'Patris' identifies the source

translation: And I send the promise of my Father upon you
